unit_17 0% 正确率 0 复习 0 错题

unit_17

推荐练习默认不提示本单元词。需要复盘时可手动显示本单元词；提交后判分，也可放弃查看答案。

50 推荐题 381 全部题目 0 弱相关题 30% 推荐答案命中比例
#1 TC TRIPLE
Having displayed his art collection in a vast modernist white space in (i)__________ former warehouse, Mr. Saatchi has chosen for his new site its polar opposite, a riverside monument to civic pomposity that once housed the local government. There is nothing (ii)__________ about the new location: the building's design is bureaucratic baroque, (iii)__________ style that is as declamatory as a task-force report and as self-regarding as a campaign speech.Having displayed his art collection in a vast modernist white space in (i)__________ former warehouse, Mr. Saatchi has chosen for his new site its polar opposite, a riverside monument to civic pomposity that once housed the local government. There is nothing (ii)__________ about the new location: the building's design is bureaucratic baroque, (iii)__________ style that is as declamatory as a task-force report and as self-regarding as a campaign speech.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　an unprepossessing; spare; a grandiose
bureaucraticbaroque
bureaucratic: standard unit headword | baroque: standard unit headword
#2 TC DOUBLE
Britain's deteriorating economy after 1945 was (i) __________ by politicians who favored the manufacturing sector over the service sector: rather than attempting to (ii) __________ the decline of manufacturing, they should have promoted service industries.Britain's deteriorating economy after 1945 was (i) __________ by politicians who favored the manufacturing sector over the service sector: rather than attempting to (ii) __________ the decline of manufacturing, they should have promoted service industries.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　mishandled; arrest
favorarrestbolsterdeteriorate
favor: standard unit headword | arrest: standard unit headword | bolster: standard unit headword | deteriorate: standard unit headword
#3 SE
Though the autobiography generally reflects the author's distinctively benign attitude toward others' foibles, some of its observations have __________ quality, revealing an underlying disdain not evident in earlier works.Though the autobiography generally reflects the author's distinctively benign attitude toward others' foibles, some of its observations have __________ quality, revealing an underlying disdain not evident in earlier works.
答案：C, E　a derisive; a contemptuous
ingenuousbenign
ingenuous: standard unit headword | benign: standard unit headword
#4 TC TRIPLE
Precipitate interest in Jennifer Francis's theory that anomalously rapid warming of the Arctic disrupts weather patterns in temperate latitudes has (i) __________ the normal life cycle of a scientific controversy. Usually data are compiled, a hypothesis is tested, and conclusions might then become news if deemed solid. Because Francis's idea links climate change to severe weather, it resonated immediately with the media and the public, and Francis has been forced to (ii) __________ before the scientific process has been (iii) __________.Precipitate interest in Jennifer Francis's theory that anomalously rapid warming of the Arctic disrupts weather patterns in temperate latitudes has (i) __________ the normal life cycle of a scientific controversy. Usually data are compiled, a hypothesis is tested, and conclusions might then become news if deemed solid. Because Francis's idea links climate change to severe weather, it resonated immediately with the media and the public, and Francis has been forced to (ii) __________ before the scientific process has been (iii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　inverted; defend her findings; concluded
hypothesisdisrupt
hypothesis: standard unit headword | disrupt: standard unit headword
#5 TC DOUBLE
Maddox presents a more (i) __________ view of Franklin than the stereotyped rendering of her as the martyr whose data were stolen. By focusing substantial attention on Franklin's achievements working on the molecular structure of coal and on virus research, Maddox (ii) __________ reducing Franklin's legacy to her involvement in the (admittedly central) scientific story of DNA.Maddox presents a more (i) __________ view of Franklin than the stereotyped rendering of her as the martyr whose data were stolen. By focusing substantial attention on Franklin's achievements working on the molecular structure of coal and on virus research, Maddox (ii) __________ reducing Franklin's legacy to her involvement in the (admittedly central) scientific story of DNA.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, D　complex; resists
deplorefacile
deplore: standard unit headword | facile: standard unit headword
#6 SE
Between the late 1800s and early 1900s, various institutional structures emerged that set researchers in scientific fields apart as a professional class and moderated disputes by __________ some kinds of knowledge as real science.Between the late 1800s and early 1900s, various institutional structures emerged that set researchers in scientific fields apart as a professional class and moderated disputes by __________ some kinds of knowledge as real science.
答案：B, F　sanctioning; endorsing
differentiatecensuresanction
differentiate: standard unit headword | censure: standard unit headword | sanction: standard unit headword
#7 TC TRIPLE
Taruskin's history of Western music, (i) __________ its remarkable length, makes no claims to comprehensiveness. Taruskin is punctilious, and justifiably proud, in emphasizing that his book is a work of history, not a survey of the repertory. In fact, he (ii) __________ criticism on grounds of omission by cheerfully admitting he has (iii) __________ such side-branch composers as Vaughan Williams, who maintained the British symphonic tradition into the mid-twentieth century.Taruskin's history of Western music, (i) __________ its remarkable length, makes no claims to comprehensiveness. Taruskin is punctilious, and justifiably proud, in emphasizing that his book is a work of history, not a survey of the repertory. In fact, he (ii) __________ criticism on grounds of omission by cheerfully admitting he has (iii) __________ such side-branch composers as Vaughan Williams, who maintained the British symphonic tradition into the mid-twentieth century.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　despite; disarms; ignored
punctilioussurvey
punctilious: standard unit headword | survey: standard unit headword

A bird’s feathers would seem to be a (i)__________ design for protecting a bird from attack by microscopic organisms. They create a warm, moist space next to the skin that could be an ideal incubator for spores. Wild birds rarely (ii)__________ skin diseases, however. The chemicals in the sebum include an array of antibacterial and anti-fungal agents that allow the bird’s skin to (iii)__________.A bird’s feathers would seem to be a (i)__________ design for protecting a bird from attack by microscopic organisms. They create a warm, moist space next to the skin that could be an ideal incubator for spores. Wild birds rarely (ii)__________ skin diseases, however. The chemicals in the sebum include an array of antibacterial and anti-fungal agents that allow the bird’s skin to (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　poor; contract; stay healthy
incubatorirritate
incubator: standard unit headword | irritate: standard unit headword
#9 TC SINGLE
The assumption that children learn about science primarily in the classroom is so __________ that few policy makers question it, despite an ever-growing body of evidence demonstrating that most science is learned outside of school.The assumption that children learn about science primarily in the classroom is so __________ that few policy makers question it, despite an ever-growing body of evidence demonstrating that most science is learned outside of school.
答案：D　pervasive
tenuouspervasive
tenuous: standard unit headword | pervasive: standard unit headword

答案：A　mercurial
disposition
disposition: standard unit headword
#11 SE
Even though his opponent is currently trying to portray him as a wild-eyed radical, voters will likely reject this charge because it does not __________ his moderate political record.Even though his opponent is currently trying to portray him as a wild-eyed radical, voters will likely reject this charge because it does not __________ his moderate political record.
答案：B, F　conform to; square with
portray
portray: standard unit headword
#12 TC DOUBLE
An architect of Koolhaas' (i) __________ might plausibly prefer designing on paper to building in concrete. Remaining within the realm of his own imagination, he need not worry about pesky clients who can dilute a project into mediocrity. But in fact what Koolhaas enjoys most is the opportunity to engage with a client. When asked if he would consider designing a house for himself, he replied that the idea bored him, that it would feel too (ii) __________. For him, the whole point of architecture is the encounter with the other.An architect of Koolhaas' (i) __________ might plausibly prefer designing on paper to building in concrete. Remaining within the realm of his own imagination, he need not worry about pesky clients who can dilute a project into mediocrity. But in fact what Koolhaas enjoys most is the opportunity to engage with a client. When asked if he would consider designing a house for himself, he replied that the idea bored him, that it would feel too (ii) __________. For him, the whole point of architecture is the encounter with the other.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　unfettered originality; solipsistic
unbridledsolipsistic
unbridled: standard unit headword | solipsistic: standard unit headword

Medieval cathedrals still stand as marvels of architecture, but as far as modern science is concerned, medieval physics and chemistry are simply irrelevant, at best a dead end, at worst the very __________ of what science is supposed to be.Medieval cathedrals still stand as marvels of architecture, but as far as modern science is concerned, medieval physics and chemistry are simply irrelevant, at best a dead end, at worst the very __________ of what science is supposed to be.
答案：E　antithesis
irrelevant
irrelevant: standard unit headword

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　efficiently; vacant
occupant

答案：B　stalwart
empathy

#17 TC TRIPLE
Why Zona Gale - a Pulitzer Prize-winning writer whose novels were both a critical and popular success - has fallen so far out of the literary canon is the fascinating subject of Deborah Lindsay Williams' recent book. Williams concludes that Gale's embrace of literary (i) __________ accounts for her eventual decline in reputation. Gale sought professional affiliation with other women writers of many stripes, and Williams contends that she has therefore been (ii) __________ by a later literary paradigm that favors (iii) __________ as a marker of artistic prowess.Why Zona Gale - a Pulitzer Prize-winning writer whose novels were both a critical and popular success - has fallen so far out of the literary canon is the fascinating subject of Deborah Lindsay Williams' recent book. Williams concludes that Gale's embrace of literary (i) __________ accounts for her eventual decline in reputation. Gale sought professional affiliation with other women writers of many stripes, and Williams contends that she has therefore been (ii) __________ by a later literary paradigm that favors (iii) __________ as a marker of artistic prowess.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, I　sisterhood; marginalized; individual exceptionalism
favorprowess
favor: standard unit headword | prowess: standard unit headword

The laboratory maze has grown ever less __________ since it was invented; instead of hoping to lose a rodent in a labyrinth, today’s scientists design mazes to elicit a few simple, easily measurable behaviors.The laboratory maze has grown ever less __________ since it was invented; instead of hoping to lose a rodent in a labyrinth, today’s scientists design mazes to elicit a few simple, easily measurable behaviors.
答案：A, B　intricate; convoluted
labyrinth
labyrinth: standard unit headword

答案：D　disingenuous

But the formula is not without its imperfections, which have grown more pronounced in recent years. The publication has always been erudite and (ii)__________ but not always lively and readable. (iii)__________, accompanied by a certain aversion to risk taking, has pervaded its pages for a long time.Unlike most other serious journals, which drain money from their owners, the Review has long been (i)__________. But the formula is not without its imperfections, which have grown more pronounced in recent years. The publication has always been erudite and (ii)__________ but not always lively and readable. (iii)__________, accompanied by a certain aversion to risk taking, has pervaded its pages for a long time.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　lucrative; authoritative; A staleness
pervade
pervade: standard unit headword

答案：C, F　restorative; tonic

Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　monolithic; differentiate various behaviors; conflating

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, H　rationale for; mentioned; energizes

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, I　made demands on; alien to; misunderstood
commentary

答案：D　porous

Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, G　discarded; immortality; death and decay

fabled: standard unit headword
#48 TC SINGLE
Without seeming unworldly, William James appeared wholly removed from the __________ of society, the conventionality of academe.Without seeming unworldly, William James appeared wholly removed from the __________ of society, the conventionality of academe.
答案：E　commonplaces
ethos
ethos: standard unit headword
#49 SE
The Great Lakes wolf is a __________, stumping scientists as to whether it is a subspecies of the gray wolf or a distinct species.The Great Lakes wolf is a __________, stumping scientists as to whether it is a subspecies of the gray wolf or a distinct species.
答案：B, F　riddle; conundrum
riddle
riddle: standard unit headword
#50 TC DOUBLE
To the avid reader of E.O. Wilson, much of his most recent book Consilience: The Unity of Knowledge will be (i) __________, as the book represents the culmination of a life spent thinking about everything from the social lives of ants to the social lives of people. Nonetheless, new thoughts have been mixed in with the old to produce a book remarkable for its (ii) __________ and ambition.To the avid reader of E.O. Wilson, much of his most recent book Consilience: The Unity of Knowledge will be (i) __________, as the book represents the culmination of a life spent thinking about everything from the social lives of ants to the social lives of people. Nonetheless, new thoughts have been mixed in with the old to produce a book remarkable for its (ii) __________ and ambition.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　predictable; purview
avid
avid: standard unit headword
#51 TC SINGLE
With the numerous opponents of the controversial new taxation measure in such a fury, anyone who publicly advocated the measure did not fail to meet with __________ usage.With the numerous opponents of the controversial new taxation measure in such a fury, anyone who publicly advocated the measure did not fail to meet with __________ usage.
答案：B　severe
dejected
dejected: standard unit headword
#52 TC TRIPLE
In 1987, Japanese chemists demonstrated that tiny particles of gold could act as (i) __________ facilitating the combination of carbon monoxide and oxygen into carbon dioxide. The idea that gold could hasten chemical reactions was (ii) __________: after all, gold is (iii) __________ in its bulk form. It has been used for millennia to make jewelry precisely because it does not tarnish by reacting with oxygen or water.In 1987, Japanese chemists demonstrated that tiny particles of gold could act as (i) __________ facilitating the combination of carbon monoxide and oxygen into carbon dioxide. The idea that gold could hasten chemical reactions was (ii) __________: after all, gold is (iii) __________ in its bulk form. It has been used for millennia to make jewelry precisely because it does not tarnish by reacting with oxygen or water.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, G　a catalyst; counterintuitive; inert
catalyst
catalyst: standard unit headword
#53 TC DOUBLE
Despite the scathing precision with which she satirizes the lives of the social aspirants and moneyed folk, the writer appears to (i)__________ being part of the world she presents as so (ii)__________.Despite the scathing precision with which she satirizes the lives of the social aspirants and moneyed folk, the writer appears to (i)__________ being part of the world she presents as so (ii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　relish; insufferable
abhor
abhor: standard unit headword
#54 SE
Scientific discovery calls for a difficult balance: intrepid advocacy of new ideas must often be __________ by the results of self-imposed trials.Scientific discovery calls for a difficult balance: intrepid advocacy of new ideas must often be __________ by the results of self-imposed trials.
答案：A, B　tempered; moderated
preempt
preempt: standard unit headword
#55 TC TRIPLE
How does one evaluate the effects of modern democracy on individuals? The first clarifying step must be to recognize that “democracy” itself can, in the abstract, (i)__________ us as we think of our society and our perception of democracy as citizens. The experience of living in a democracy (ii)__________ each generation. A feature of democratic is that something that benefits us in one generation may no longer be a benefit to the next. Thus, experiencing democracy in the twenty-first-century is (iii)__________ the political order our ancestors cherished in 1901.How does one evaluate the effects of modern democracy on individuals? The first clarifying step must be to recognize that “democracy” itself can, in the abstract, (i)__________ us as we think of our society and our perception of democracy as citizens. The experience of living in a democracy (ii)__________ each generation. A feature of democratic is that something that benefits us in one generation may no longer be a benefit to the next. Thus, experiencing democracy in the twenty-first-century is (iii)__________ the political order our ancestors cherished in 1901.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　mislead; changes for; radically different from
abet
abet: standard unit headword
#56 SE
Peoples’ decisions about childbearing depend on innumerable personal considerations and societal factors, yet even knowing this, demographers are often __________: their projections of birth rates frequently turn out to be embarrassingly at odds with reality.Peoples’ decisions about childbearing depend on innumerable personal considerations and societal factors, yet even knowing this, demographers are often __________: their projections of birth rates frequently turn out to be embarrassingly at odds with reality.
答案：B, F　flummoxed; confounded

答案：C　an animadversion

While early biographies of Florence Nightingale tended to be quite __________, Lytton Strachey's irreverent 1918 essay about her ushered in a new era, making it acceptable, even fashionable, to criticize her.While early biographies of Florence Nightingale tended to be quite __________, Lytton Strachey's irreverent 1918 essay about her ushered in a new era, making it acceptable, even fashionable, to criticize her.
答案：B　sycophantic

Even when the (ii)__________ meant that playwriting did not bring personal fame, the work nevertheless enabled them to present their own views to the public and offered the possibility of acquiring capital.As Ellen Donkin explains, in 18th century England, writing plays (i)__________ women. Even when the (ii)__________ meant that playwriting did not bring personal fame, the work nevertheless enabled them to present their own views to the public and offered the possibility of acquiring capital.

Blank 2

答案：C, F　denounces; lambastes

#63 TC TRIPLE
Firebaugh and Beck contend that economic development improves the overall well-being of people within developing countries. However, other scholars emphasize the (i)__________ of this view, empirically demonstrating that while economic development does in fact contribute to the well-being of the population of developing countries, the magnitude of development's positive effects on well-being has (ii)__________. In other words, these scholars suggest that (iii)__________ economic development and human well-being is taking place in developing countries.Firebaugh and Beck contend that economic development improves the overall well-being of people within developing countries. However, other scholars emphasize the (i)__________ of this view, empirically demonstrating that while economic development does in fact contribute to the well-being of the population of developing countries, the magnitude of development's positive effects on well-being has (ii)__________. In other words, these scholars suggest that (iii)__________ economic development and human well-being is taking place in developing countries.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, G　limitation; decreased over time; a decoupling of

答案：A　zealous
abhorrent

答案：C, E　sacrosanct; inviolable

答案：B, E　prominence; salience

答案：B, C　extirpated; eliminated

Blank 2

This seems (i)__________ conclusion in hindsight, but its implication—that geological processes in the distant past must have (ii)__________ these very same laws—was (iii)__________ geologists in the eighteenth and nineteenth centuries.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　an obvious; followed; revolutionary for

Not surprisingly, this style works better in short-story form than in novels, and the story collection I Wish Someone Were Waiting for me Somewhere (ii)__________ her attempt to (iii)__________ it in a novel, as in, for example, Someone I Loved.Anna Gavalda’s fiction is appealing largely because of her writing styles, which is certainly (i)__________. It is heavy on dialogue (and light on embellishment), features very short sections (of often only a few sentence) in rapid succession, and in general favors an unadorned directness. Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3

#100 TC TRIPLE
Unlike most other serious journals, which drain money from their owners, the Review has long been (i)__________. But the formula is not without its imperfections, which have grown more pronounced in recent years. The publication has always been erudite and (ii)__________ but not always lively and readable. (iii)__________, accompanied by a certain aversion to risk taking, has pervaded its pages for a long time.Unlike most other serious journals, which drain money from their owners, the Review has long been (i)__________. But the formula is not without its imperfections, which have grown more pronounced in recent years. The publication has always been erudite and (ii)__________ but not always lively and readable. (iii)__________, accompanied by a certain aversion to risk taking, has pervaded its pages for a long time.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　lucrative; authoritative; a staleness
pervade
pervade: standard unit headword
#101 TC SINGLE
Anthropologist Jane Goodall was __________ in her determination to anthropomorphize the animals she observed with such empathy, and so resisted her editors' attempt to recast her descriptions in more dispassionate language.Anthropologist Jane Goodall was __________ in her determination to anthropomorphize the animals she observed with such empathy, and so resisted her editors' attempt to recast her descriptions in more dispassionate language.
答案：B　stalwart
empathy
empathy: standard unit headword
#102 TC TRIPLE
Both very good and very bad books are easy to review. Praise and (i)__________ come easily. But what of books that contain a muddle of virtues and vices? Here the reviewer's task is more (ii)__________: the author's useful and thought-provoking observations need to be (iii)__________ the useless and tedious.Both very good and very bad books are easy to review. Praise and (i)__________ come easily. But what of books that contain a muddle of virtues and vices? Here the reviewer's task is more (ii)__________: the author's useful and thought-provoking observations need to be (iii)__________ the useless and tedious.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　censure; demanding; sifted from
censure
censure: standard unit headword

In science education, it is important to differentiate between inaccurate ideas that are conceptually (i)__________ and understandings that are inaccurate, and yet can (ii)__________ learning of more sophisticated understandings. The former are simply wrong, the latter can be seen as incomplete, overly simplistic, or tied to only a few limited contexts.In science education, it is important to differentiate between inaccurate ideas that are conceptually (i)__________ and understandings that are inaccurate, and yet can (ii)__________ learning of more sophisticated understandings. The former are simply wrong, the latter can be seen as incomplete, overly simplistic, or tied to only a few limited contexts.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　unproductive; foster
differentiate
differentiate: standard unit headword
#104 TC SINGLE
While many books have emphasized the differences between Delaney and his father - and his need to differentiate himself from his father - Delaney takes pains in this memoir to __________ his closeness with his dad.While many books have emphasized the differences between Delaney and his father - and his need to differentiate himself from his father - Delaney takes pains in this memoir to __________ his closeness with his dad.
答案：A　underscore
differentiate
differentiate: standard unit headword

答案：A, C　discerned by; discovered by

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　intransigent; uncompromising
intransigent

Thus, theories and tools developed to study implicit cognition may increase our understanding of the complex interplay between culture and individuals.Cultures can shape attitudes and beliefs in ways that (i)__________ conscious awareness or control; in other words, cultural orientations may develop from processes that do not entail (ii)__________ participation, and cultures may pervade subtle psychological dynamics in ways that individuals may not be able to (iii)__________. Thus, theories and tools developed to study implicit cognition may increase our understanding of the complex interplay between culture and individuals.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, G　operate outside of; active; report

答案：C　predominant

survey: standard unit headword
#109 SE

答案：C, F　irrelevant; extraneous

Any given author style, in other words, is regarded as (i)__________ and (ii)__________ every other authors’ writing style.

Blank 2

答案：C　disingenuous

For instance, he deplores the Royal Shakespeare Company's eight-and-a-half hour version of Nicholas Nickelby, which many found (ii) __________.

Given So's (iii)__________ many of the players in Hong Kong politics, it is surprising that he did not use interviews and other forms of direct research to delve further into the motivations, strategies, and tactics of participants.A minor criticism of the book, which is (i)__________ an understanding of the difficulty of doing direct research in Hong Kong's, is that So relied on secondary sources to tell the story of Hong Kong's political development, with previous histories of the period (ii)__________ his research. Blank 2

It is heavy on dialogue (and light on embellishment), features very short sections (of often only a few sentence) in rapid succession, and in general favors an unadorned directness. Not surprisingly, this style works better in short-story form than in novels, and the story collection I Wish Someone Were Waiting for Me Somewhere (ii)__________ her attempt to (iii)__________ it in novel, as in, for example, Someone I Loved.Anna Gavalda's fiction is appealing largely because of her writing styles, which is certainly (i)__________. It is heavy on dialogue (and light on embellishment), features very short sections (of often only a few sentence) in rapid succession, and in general favors an unadorned directness. Blank 2
Blank 3

As it is, newcomers to the subject will have little (iii)__________ the afterlife enjoyed by ancient philosophy in the period 1600-1750.In this single volume, Kenny aims to survey for the general reader all of ancient philosophy; understandably, space in such a book is (i)__________, and he is not to be faulted for minor omissions. Blank 2

Blank 2
Blank 3

#152 SE
As astronomers do not yet have a good understanding of the fundamental nature of dark matter, it should not be a surprise that astronomy students’ ideas about dark matter are, at best, __________.As astronomers do not yet have a good understanding of the fundamental nature of dark matter, it should not be a surprise that astronomy students’ ideas about dark matter are, at best, __________.
答案：A, B　superficial; sketchy
sketchy
sketchy: standard unit headword

The survey showed widespread misgivings among Asian business leaders, with even those who have prospered most in the recent past not being __________ about their future prospects.The survey showed widespread misgivings among Asian business leaders, with even those who have prospered most in the recent past not being __________ about their future prospects.
答案：C　sanguine
survey
survey: standard unit headword

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　technological; germane to
implication

Because leadership in the computer hacker group is extremely (i)__________, when law enforcement agencies arrested several prominent members, taking them offline, the group (ii)__________. Instead, it continues to operate, sometimes under its own name, sometimes under other names. The names are (iii)__________, as are the members of the group: anyone, anywhere, is free to work behind its mask.Because leadership in the computer hacker group is extremely (i)__________, when law enforcement agencies arrested several prominent members, taking them offline, the group (ii)__________. Instead, it continues to operate, sometimes under its own name, sometimes under other names. The names are (iii)__________, as are the members of the group: anyone, anywhere, is free to work behind its mask.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, H　fluid; did not collapse; fungible
arrest
arrest: standard unit headword

In American art the line between the good and the goods is not a hard-and-fast boundary, for in a commercial society the membrane that separates spirit and store is always __________.In American art the line between the good and the goods is not a hard-and-fast boundary, for in a commercial society the membrane that separates spirit and store is always __________.
答案：A, C　porous; permeable

Confounding the perception of needlework as __________, the Oxburgh embroideries, executed by a sixteenth-century Scottish queen and her attendant, incorporate text and imagery from books by distinguished scholars of the time.Confounding the perception of needlework as __________, the Oxburgh embroideries, executed by a sixteenth-century Scottish queen and her attendant, incorporate text and imagery from books by distinguished scholars of the time.
答案：E　a vacuous pastime

Blank 2
答案：B, D　shortcomings; remedy

These climatologists say the evidence of a tie between carbon dioxide and planetary warming over the last few centuries is so (ii)__________ that any longer-term evidence against such a link must somehow be (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, G　irrelevant; compelling; tainted

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　umbrage; collectively overthrow

答案：B, F　flummoxed; confounded

As theories, quantum field theory and theory of general relativity have been astonishingly (i) __________ that no experiment yet has shown a crack in either of them, even though both of them (ii) __________ wildly counterintuitive properties in physical reality. And yet even proponents note that the two theories are (iii) __________, a fact that has disallowed theorists--at least so far--from incorporating both into a self-consistent theory of quantum gravity.As theories, quantum field theory and theory of general relativity have been astonishingly (i) __________ that no experiment yet has shown a crack in either of them, even though both of them (ii) __________ wildly counterintuitive properties in physical reality. And yet even proponents note that the two theories are (iii) __________, a fact that has disallowed theorists--at least so far--from incorporating both into a self-consistent theory of quantum gravity.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, H　successful; posit; deeply at odds with each other

incorporate: standard unit headword

答案：D, F　rudimentary; sketchy

Blank 3

By the 1970s many scientists had become (ii) __________ their hypothesis, and it was largely (iii) __________ as a new set of theories claiming that language and thought are universal came to dominate the field.

Blank 3
答案：B, E, I　excitement; disenchanted with; abandoned

According to linguists and cognitive scientists, the human mind specializes in producing analogies. Part of the reason cognitive scientists believe analogy is a (i) __________ cognitive process is that drawing analogies requires some sort of similarity mapping of one concept onto another, and similarity mapping is a component of the inductive thinking we depend on. Another argument for the (ii) __________ of analogical reasoning in human reasoning is that metaphors, which are linguistically embedded analogies, are (iii) __________ language.According to linguists and cognitive scientists, the human mind specializes in producing analogies. Part of the reason cognitive scientists believe analogy is a (i) __________ cognitive process is that drawing analogies requires some sort of similarity mapping of one concept onto another, and similarity mapping is a component of the inductive thinking we depend on. Another argument for the (ii) __________ of analogical reasoning in human reasoning is that metaphors, which are linguistically embedded analogies, are (iii) __________ language.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, G　fundamental; centrality; pervasive in
pervasive

答案：A, E　insecure; precarious

#206 TC TRIPLE
Although the impression that quantum mechanics is limited to the microworld (i) __________ the public understanding of science, it is (ii) __________. Quantum effects may be harder to see in the macroworld, but experiments have now confirmed that these effects are (iii) __________ than anyone ever suspected.Although the impression that quantum mechanics is limited to the microworld (i) __________ the public understanding of science, it is (ii) __________. Quantum effects may be harder to see in the macroworld, but experiments have now confirmed that these effects are (iii) __________ than anyone ever suspected.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　permeates; erroneous; more pervasive
pervasive
pervasive: standard unit headword
#207 SE
Though Edmund certainly had a dignified bearing and made a great first impression, those who became acquainted with him soon realized he had an essentially __________ nature.Though Edmund certainly had a dignified bearing and made a great first impression, those who became acquainted with him soon realized he had an essentially __________ nature.
答案：A, F　pugnacious; truculent
punctilious
punctilious: standard unit headword
#208 TC SINGLE
Geologists suggest that as the most productive oil reservoirs begin to dry up, the expensive cost and high risk of drilling in the marginal area becomes less __________ and more acceptable.Geologists suggest that as the most productive oil reservoirs begin to dry up, the expensive cost and high risk of drilling in the marginal area becomes less __________ and more acceptable.
答案：A　onerous
benign
benign: standard unit headword
#209 TC DOUBLE
The philosopher held that art must perform a paradoxical task, simultaneously extricating itself from the (i) __________ even while (ii) __________ the realities of ordinary experience.The philosopher held that art must perform a paradoxical task, simultaneously extricating itself from the (i) __________ even while (ii) __________ the realities of ordinary experience.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, F　quotidian; forthrightly addressing
address
address: standard unit headword
#210 TC TRIPLE
Perhaps the best one can say of this new book of criticism is that it is scintillatingly (i) __________. The author is adept at dealing with trivial details in the poets he studies, but he is quite (ii) __________ when it comes to illuminating the overall vision. The verbal intricacy and panache of his writing contrast with its conceptual (iii) __________. If there are very few authentic ideas in the poems under consideration, there is also a shortage of them in the author's commentary.Perhaps the best one can say of this new book of criticism is that it is scintillatingly (i) __________. The author is adept at dealing with trivial details in the poets he studies, but he is quite (ii) __________ when it comes to illuminating the overall vision. The verbal intricacy and panache of his writing contrast with its conceptual (iii) __________. If there are very few authentic ideas in the poems under consideration, there is also a shortage of them in the author's commentary.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, I　shallow; indifferent; poverty
commentary
commentary: standard unit headword
#211 SE
Since it was committed to the idea of science as an international, politically neutral enterprise, the Royal Society of London refused to __________ members from enemy nations during world wars of the twentieth century.Since it was committed to the idea of science as an international, politically neutral enterprise, the Royal Society of London refused to __________ members from enemy nations during world wars of the twentieth century.
答案：B, D　expel; oust
sanction
sanction: standard unit headword
#212 TC DOUBLE
When searching for an object, Argentine ants adjust their (i) __________ based on (ii) __________: if they detect many other ants around them, they turn around; if there are only a few ants, they walk in a straighter line.When searching for an object, Argentine ants adjust their (i) __________ based on (ii) __________: if they detect many other ants around them, they turn around; if there are only a few ants, they walk in a straighter line.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　orientation; proximity
proximity
proximity: standard unit headword

It is a paradox of the Victorians that they were both __________ and, through the empire, cosmopolitan.It is a paradox of the Victorians that they were both __________ and, through the empire, cosmopolitan.
答案：B　insular
intransigent
intransigent: standard unit headword
#214 TC TRIPLE
Although Dostoyevsky was not the (i) __________ portrayed by some biographers, at least implicitly guilty of the terrible crimes he depicted in his novels, he was notoriously (ii) __________. Indeed, his attitude toward people who questioned him about his work was anything but friendly. No such problems bother students of Chekhov. On the contrary, it is difficult to think of a writer of equal fame and importance who, on close inspection, proves to be such a (iii) __________ human being.Although Dostoyevsky was not the (i) __________ portrayed by some biographers, at least implicitly guilty of the terrible crimes he depicted in his novels, he was notoriously (ii) __________. Indeed, his attitude toward people who questioned him about his work was anything but friendly. No such problems bother students of Chekhov. On the contrary, it is difficult to think of a writer of equal fame and importance who, on close inspection, proves to be such a (iii) __________ human being.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, G　monster; prickly; sympathetic
portray
portray: standard unit headword
#215 SE
Despite a tendency to be overtly __________, the poetry does not consist solely of pious sentiments: it sparks the imagination and provides lively entertainment.Despite a tendency to be overtly __________, the poetry does not consist solely of pious sentiments: it sparks the imagination and provides lively entertainment.
答案：A, D　preachy; sanctimonious
disingenuous
disingenuous: standard unit headword
#216 TC DOUBLE
A skeptic is someone who does not believe a claim just because someone makes it but instead tests it against evidence. Sooner or later, if the evidence is solid, then the skeptic must acknowledge the claim's legitimacy. Skeptics are (i) __________ deniers, who are ideologically committed to a position and (ii) __________ in the face of evidence.A skeptic is someone who does not believe a claim just because someone makes it but instead tests it against evidence. Sooner or later, if the evidence is solid, then the skeptic must acknowledge the claim's legitimacy. Skeptics are (i) __________ deniers, who are ideologically committed to a position and (ii) __________ in the face of evidence.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　distinct from; intransigent
intransigent
intransigent: standard unit headword

答案：B　sanguine

Despite Goody's contention that a kind of literacy line cuts through history, dividing oral from print cultures, it seems that traditional tale-telling can (iii) __________ long after a print culture is established.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, I　tenacious; collapse; flourish

答案：E, F　disposition; proclivity

答案：B　obscure

Yet science and scientists rarely (i) __________ in movies involving trials. When a scientist is negatively portrayed, it is often because the legal process is represented as having (ii) __________ science; when a scientist is positively portrayed, it is often because science is represented as superior to law, as (iii) __________ law's rhetorical and institutional instabilities.Because popular culture often portrays lawyers as shady and scientists as eccentric, we might expect cinematic depictions of scientists employed as expert witnesses in court cases to reflect badly on both law and science. Yet science and scientists rarely (i) __________ in movies involving trials. When a scientist is negatively portrayed, it is often because the legal process is represented as having (ii) __________ science; when a scientist is positively portrayed, it is often because science is represented as superior to law, as (iii) __________ law's rhetorical and institutional instabilities.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　suffer reputational losses; tainted; a counterweight to

#230 TC TRIPLE
For a philosopher of the analytic tradition, Williams' approach is somewhat (i) __________. Unlike most analytic philosophers, Williams has not kept himself (ii) __________ the rest of the humanities; in fact, he wishes to address colleagues in other fields in terms that will make sense to them. In particular, he objects to the (iii) __________ character of analytic philosophy: the amount it tries to accomplish by conceptual analysis and a priori argument alone. Williams is convinced that philosophy must use history, including historical imagination, to understand and defend values of any kind.For a philosopher of the analytic tradition, Williams' approach is somewhat (i) __________. Unlike most analytic philosophers, Williams has not kept himself (ii) __________ the rest of the humanities; in fact, he wishes to address colleagues in other fields in terms that will make sense to them. In particular, he objects to the (iii) __________ character of analytic philosophy: the amount it tries to accomplish by conceptual analysis and a priori argument alone. Williams is convinced that philosophy must use history, including historical imagination, to understand and defend values of any kind.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　unorthodox; aloof from; strongly ahistorical
address
address: standard unit headword

答案：A, F　unassuming; modest

(i) __________ is a wonderful thing, but it does not defeat mental (ii) __________ because all attempts to grasp the subjective experience of another are interpretations, not acts of merging.

In this view, the flesh-and-blood men and women involved--their personalities and histories--are (ii) __________ their philosophical claims. 答案：C, D, H　an impersonal; irrelevant to; inseparable from

But once the paradigm (ii)__________, the academic incentives shift in the opposite direction: research results are more likely to be considered worthy of publication when they (iii)__________ what has become the established view.When a new scientific model emerges, research studies (i)__________ that paradigm tend to dominate in the scientific literature: the process of selecting articles for publication is tilted toward positive results. 答案：B, E, I　affirming; has become entrenched; undermine

Blank 2
答案：A, D　didactic; amount to
pervade
pervade: standard unit headword
#259 TC TRIPLE
African civilizations have long been depicted as (i) __________ cultural progress initiated by ancient Mediterranean civilizations. However, evidence of significant contact between the ancient Mediterranean and sub-Saharan Africa has been (ii) __________, rendering these exogenous explanations (iii) __________.African civilizations have long been depicted as (i) __________ cultural progress initiated by ancient Mediterranean civilizations. However, evidence of significant contact between the ancient Mediterranean and sub-Saharan Africa has been (ii) __________, rendering these exogenous explanations (iii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　recipients of; meager; tenuous
tenuous
tenuous: standard unit headword
#260 TC SINGLE
By the time computers were being manufactured for commercial use, programming was recognized as __________: the work was tedious and slow, and programming projects became notorious for running over budget and behind schedule.By the time computers were being manufactured for commercial use, programming was recognized as __________: the work was tedious and slow, and programming projects became notorious for running over budget and behind schedule.
答案：E　a costly bottleneck
irrelevant
irrelevant: standard unit headword
#261 TC DOUBLE
In those days, most county prosecutors' offices were extremely (i) __________ operations, with few meaningful contacts beyond the county's borders. Thus, it is (ii) __________ that Kaus, during her tenure as county prosecutor, reached all the way to the nation's capital in order to overhaul the office.In those days, most county prosecutors' offices were extremely (i) __________ operations, with few meaningful contacts beyond the county's borders. Thus, it is (ii) __________ that Kaus, during her tenure as county prosecutor, reached all the way to the nation's capital in order to overhaul the office.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　insular; noteworthy
overhaul
overhaul: standard unit headword
#262 SE
In his article, Bazerman highlights Edison's __________ manipulation of the press, including gifts of stock to friendly reporters.In his article, Bazerman highlights Edison's __________ manipulation of the press, including gifts of stock to friendly reporters.
答案：D, F　brazen; barefaced
brazen
brazen: standard unit headword
#263 TC TRIPLE
Wolosky claims that Ella Wheeler Wilcox joined other women poets such as Julia Ward Howe, Frances Harper, and Charlotte Perkins Gilman in critiquing materialism and possessive individualism. Wolosky’s description (i)__________ the poetry of Gilman, Howe, and Harper, but it is not entirely (ii)__________ in the case of Wilcox, who hardly (iii)__________ the materialism of her time. Rather, Wilcox seems to have embraced the amassing of private property.Wolosky claims that Ella Wheeler Wilcox joined other women poets such as Julia Ward Howe, Frances Harper, and Charlotte Perkins Gilman in critiquing materialism and possessive individualism. Wolosky’s description (i)__________ the poetry of Gilman, Howe, and Harper, but it is not entirely (ii)__________ in the case of Wilcox, who hardly (iii)__________ the materialism of her time. Rather, Wilcox seems to have embraced the amassing of private property.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　fits; apt; impugned
conflate
conflate: standard unit headword

答案：C　mawkish
benign
benign: standard unit headword

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, D　convinced of; economic utility

答案：C, F　abet; support

Blank 2

On such occasions patriotic sentiment and national pride wrapped in the panoply of history to manufacture a mythical past that is serviceable for public (iii)__________.

Blank 2

答案：B, E　abstruse; recondite
recondite

Gravity is the __________ of the four fundamental forces existing in nature, the others being the electromagnetic force, and the strong and weak nuclear forces; yet over large distances it is gravity that dominates.Gravity is the __________ of the four fundamental forces existing in nature, the others being the electromagnetic force, and the strong and weak nuclear forces; yet over large distances it is gravity that dominates.
答案：D　least potent

While Americans are portrayed as (ii) __________ in this version of the story, research reveals that most listeners knew the program was fictional.The 1938 "War of the Worlds" fiasco, in which millions of people supposedly believed that a fictional radio program about a Martian invasion was true, is held up as an example of the (i) __________ of the American public. While Americans are portrayed as (ii) __________ in this version of the story, research reveals that most listeners knew the program was fictional.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　credulity; gullible

#275 TC TRIPLE
The essential difference between writing nonfiction and writing fiction is that the artist can (i)__________ a completed vision of the world, while the journalist never can, the real world being always (ii)__________. Art provides freedom from the bewildering complexities of constant change. Indeed, it is the very (iii)__________ of well-wrought fiction that can sometimes make it feel more real than reality.The essential difference between writing nonfiction and writing fiction is that the artist can (i)__________ a completed vision of the world, while the journalist never can, the real world being always (ii)__________. Art provides freedom from the bewildering complexities of constant change. Indeed, it is the very (iii)__________ of well-wrought fiction that can sometimes make it feel more real than reality.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, I　realize; unfinished; clarity

答案：B, D　ubiquitous; pervasive

Reweighting the system to favor creditors could diminish that spirit, she warns, and make it harder for entrepreneurs to try again after a failure.

But two and a half millennia later, when classical music is generally seen merely as a lifestyle accessory, Plato's conception seems (ii)__________. To be sure, there are still people who consider classical music to be of (iii)__________ cultural importance, but few of them are able to articulate this convincingly.For Plato the art of music was so (i)__________ moral and political reality that any alteration to music system would necessarily require a corresponding political shift. But two and a half millennia later, when classical music is generally seen merely as a lifestyle accessory, Plato's conception seems (ii)__________. To be sure, there are still people who consider classical music to be of (iii)__________ cultural importance, but few of them are able to articulate this convincingly.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　firmly anchored in; absurd; profound

But once the paradigm (ii)__________, the academic incentives shift in the opposite direction: research results are more often considered worthy of publication when they (iii)__________ what has become the established view.

It retains this potency partly because its control of the nation's banking system is (i) __________ governmental interference, and thus its actions remain largely (ii) __________.The national bank has been uncommonly powerful in comparison to its counterparts in other nations. Media reports regularly imply that industry support of scientific work is alone sufficient to (ii)__________ that research. Media reports regularly imply that industry support of scientific work is alone sufficient to (ii)__________ that research. Even though the source of funding has been determined to be a less significant cause of bias than other factors, industry support suffices, in the minds of many people, to (iii)__________ the credibility of scientific work.

Blank 2

答案：C, F　specious; unfounded

abhor: standard unit headword
#311 TC TRIPLE
Marie Tharp's mapping of the ocean floor, which was based on a mass of previously unanalyzed measurements of ocean depth, did not depend on (i)__________ but on (ii)__________: she (iii)__________ the sea floor's contours by marrying the voluminous but incomplete data with her knowledge of geology.Marie Tharp's mapping of the ocean floor, which was based on a mass of previously unanalyzed measurements of ocean depth, did not depend on (i)__________ but on (ii)__________: she (iii)__________ the sea floor's contours by marrying the voluminous but incomplete data with her knowledge of geology.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, G　rote work; extrapolation; inferred
prowess
prowess: standard unit headword
#312 TC DOUBLE
As Ellen Donkin explains, in eighteenth-century England, writing plays (i) __________ women. Even when the (ii) __________ meant that playwriting did not bring personal fame, the work nevertheless enabled them to present their own views to the public and offered the possibility of acquiring capital.As Ellen Donkin explains, in eighteenth-century England, writing plays (i) __________ women. Even when the (ii) __________ meant that playwriting did not bring personal fame, the work nevertheless enabled them to present their own views to the public and offered the possibility of acquiring capital.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　empowered; use of a pseudonym
remuneration
remuneration: standard unit headword
#313 SE
Some have argued that naming scientific discoveries after the people who make them can __________ scientific progress, because the nomenclature lacks useful clarity, having no relationship to the underlying principles that govern nature.Some have argued that naming scientific discoveries after the people who make them can __________ scientific progress, because the nomenclature lacks useful clarity, having no relationship to the underlying principles that govern nature.
答案：A, F　hamper; impede
abet
abet: standard unit headword
#314 TC TRIPLE
The essential difference between writing nonfiction and fiction is that the artist can (i)__________ a completed vision of the world, while the journalist never can, the real world being always (ii)__________. Art provides freedom from the bewildering complexities of constant change. Indeed, it is the very (iii)__________ of well-wrought fiction that can sometimes make it feel more real than reality.The essential difference between writing nonfiction and fiction is that the artist can (i)__________ a completed vision of the world, while the journalist never can, the real world being always (ii)__________. Art provides freedom from the bewildering complexities of constant change. Indeed, it is the very (iii)__________ of well-wrought fiction that can sometimes make it feel more real than reality.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, I　realize; unfinished; clarity
divert
divert: standard unit headword
#315 TC DOUBLE
Nordhaus predicts that in the future we will increasingly be (i) __________ ecological problems like global warming rather than (ii) __________ them. We may, for example, make some headway in limiting emissions that contribute to warming, but much of our work will be in adapting to ecological problems and alleviating their effects.Nordhaus predicts that in the future we will increasingly be (i) __________ ecological problems like global warming rather than (ii) __________ them. We may, for example, make some headway in limiting emissions that contribute to warming, but much of our work will be in adapting to ecological problems and alleviating their effects.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　managing; solving

Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　tendentious; selective; omitting

ingenuous: standard unit headword

答案：A, D　preeminence; hegemony

Witness the (ii) __________ a program that allows us to create computer greeting cards for the entire year in one sitting.

Blank 2

#319 TC TRIPLE
Computers' triumph in chess had been engineered not by creating machines that (i)__________ human thought, as most experts in artificial intelligence had expected, but by perfecting machines that played like machines. The analogy with flight is (ii)__________: as long as people tried to fly by imitating birds, attaching wings to their arms and flapping madly, they were (iii)__________ to fail. Once engineering escaped the paradigm of the familiar, however, people were soon flying much faster than birds.Computers' triumph in chess had been engineered not by creating machines that (i)__________ human thought, as most experts in artificial intelligence had expected, but by perfecting machines that played like machines. The analogy with flight is (ii)__________: as long as people tried to fly by imitating birds, attaching wings to their arms and flapping madly, they were (iii)__________ to fail. Once engineering escaped the paradigm of the familiar, however, people were soon flying much faster than birds.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, I　aped; instructive; doomed

答案：C, E　eradicate; extirpate

答案：B, D, I　hypostatize; did not posit; regarded it as a process

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　innocuous; baneful

答案：B, F, G　adopt; threatened to compromise; distinctiveness

答案：C, E　Projecting; Extrapolating

The infrasonic calls usually have a component that is (i)__________, but these higher frequency aspects of the calls (ii)__________ very quickly with distance, so that in the wild, human observers are often (iii)__________ sudden coordinated movements of large groups of elephants when no signal was apparent to the observer.Elephants are known to use a wide variety of calls, many of them infrasonic (below the level of human hearing), in coordinating group behavior. The infrasonic calls usually have a component that is (i)__________, but these higher frequency aspects of the calls (ii)__________ very quickly with distance, so that in the wild, human observers are often (iii)__________ sudden coordinated movements of large groups of elephants when no signal was apparent to the observer.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, I　humanly audible; attenuate; astonished by

irrelevant: standard unit headword
#361 SE
Critics have raved about the beauty of Peck's prose, but in his fiction, at least, the diction has only gotten more portentous with time, the writing more overwrought, and the symbolism more __________.Critics have raved about the beauty of Peck's prose, but in his fiction, at least, the diction has only gotten more portentous with time, the writing more overwrought, and the symbolism more __________.
答案：C, E　ungainly; awkward
irritate
irritate: standard unit headword
#362 TC TRIPLE
In his perceptive study of the emergence and evolution of modern urban planning in Paris, Nicholas Papayanis challenges the widespread view of Baron Georges-Eugene Haussmann as (i) __________ figure in modern conceptions of the city. Although Papayanis (ii) __________ the importance of Haussmann's accomplishments and public work projects in the creation of modern Paris, he nevertheless argues that the forms of city planning and approaches to urban space these works display are less (iii) __________ than has hitherto been thought.In his perceptive study of the emergence and evolution of modern urban planning in Paris, Nicholas Papayanis challenges the widespread view of Baron Georges-Eugene Haussmann as (i) __________ figure in modern conceptions of the city. Although Papayanis (ii) __________ the importance of Haussmann's accomplishments and public work projects in the creation of modern Paris, he nevertheless argues that the forms of city planning and approaches to urban space these works display are less (iii) __________ than has hitherto been thought.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, I　the seminal; concedes; original
irrelevant
irrelevant: standard unit headword
#363 TC DOUBLE
The quest for new drugs to deter the aging process represents a new faith among some researchers that aging is not immutable. This (i) __________, however, is not (ii) __________. Evolutionary biologists have strong reasons to suppose that the human life span cannot be altered in any easy way.The quest for new drugs to deter the aging process represents a new faith among some researchers that aging is not immutable. This (i) __________, however, is not (ii) __________. Evolutionary biologists have strong reasons to suppose that the human life span cannot be altered in any easy way.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　optimism; fully shared
sophistication
sophistication: standard unit headword
#364 TC TRIPLE
Even mild criticism of public radio news reporting seems (i) __________, because the (ii) __________ such programming regularly receives is well deserved: the reporting is (iii) __________. In recent years, though, it has become clear that, for all its excellence, public radio will continue to lose viewers to its flashy but superficial rivals.Even mild criticism of public radio news reporting seems (i) __________, because the (ii) __________ such programming regularly receives is well deserved: the reporting is (iii) __________. In recent years, though, it has become clear that, for all its excellence, public radio will continue to lose viewers to its flashy but superficial rivals.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　churlish; approbation; consistently perspicacious
facile
facile: standard unit headword
#365 SE
Fedigan argues that, in actuality, ethologists who claim to __________ anthropomorphism often end up simply substituting one set of anthropomorphic terms for another.Fedigan argues that, in actuality, ethologists who claim to __________ anthropomorphism often end up simply substituting one set of anthropomorphic terms for another.
答案：A, F　eschew; avoid
denounce
denounce: standard unit headword
#366 TC DOUBLE
In the author's view, the idea that individuals have different learning styles, such as auditory or kinesthetic, is (i) __________; it may be highly intuitive to many, but it is simply (ii) __________.In the author's view, the idea that individuals have different learning styles, such as auditory or kinesthetic, is (i) __________; it may be highly intuitive to many, but it is simply (ii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, D　a pernicious myth; erroneous
hypothesis
hypothesis: standard unit headword
#367 SE
Contrary to certain recent analyses that paint a dire portrait of soil loss from farmland, a new study of surveying data reaching back to the 1930s shows that erosion rates have been steadily __________.Contrary to certain recent analyses that paint a dire portrait of soil loss from farmland, a new study of surveying data reaching back to the 1930s shows that erosion rates have been steadily __________.
答案：B, E　waning; declining
surveydeteriorate
survey: standard unit headword | deteriorate: standard unit headword
#368 TC TRIPLE
A bird's feathers would seem to be a (i) __________ design for protecting a bird from attack by microscopic organisms. They create a warm, moist space next to the skin that could be an ideal incubator for spores. Wild birds rarely (ii) __________ skin diseases, however. The chemicals in the sebum include an array of antibacterial and antifungal agents that allow the bird's skin to (iii) __________.A bird's feathers would seem to be a (i) __________ design for protecting a bird from attack by microscopic organisms. They create a warm, moist space next to the skin that could be an ideal incubator for spores. Wild birds rarely (ii) __________ skin diseases, however. The chemicals in the sebum include an array of antibacterial and antifungal agents that allow the bird's skin to (iii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　poor; contract; stay healthy

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　coalesce; merge

#370 TC TRIPLE
The amount of water flowing through United States streams has (i)__________ during this century, although without giving rise to more frequent floods, according to a new study by the United States Geological Survey. The country appears to be getting (ii)__________ even as its stream flow becomes less (iii)__________.The amount of water flowing through United States streams has (i)__________ during this century, although without giving rise to more frequent floods, according to a new study by the United States Geological Survey. The country appears to be getting (ii)__________ even as its stream flow becomes less (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　steadily increased; wetter; destructive

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　an open; incompatible with

If she (ii) __________ the reasoning of proponents of one side, that did not mean that she rejected their position.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　inferable from; challenged

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, D　unaltered in the process; reflective of

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　resolute; qualified

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　not uncommon; exotic

#378 TC DOUBLE
Fashion designer Elsa Schiaparelli was just nineteen years old when Futurism erupted, but she later heeded that violent, belligerent art movement's call in the (i) __________ of her broad-shouldered suits, the rawness of her furs and embroidery, and her (ii) __________ attitude, which her contemporaries described as "hard chic," toward any simpering or mincing in fashion.Fashion designer Elsa Schiaparelli was just nineteen years old when Futurism erupted, but she later heeded that violent, belligerent art movement's call in the (i) __________ of her broad-shouldered suits, the rawness of her furs and embroidery, and her (ii) __________ attitude, which her contemporaries described as "hard chic," toward any simpering or mincing in fashion.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　swagger; pugnacious
ingenuous
ingenuous: standard unit headword
#379 TC DOUBLE
Science is so good at what it does that many people feel a (i) __________ to apply it to social and political issues, yet experience shows that science may add little to or even (ii) __________ social and political questions.Science is so good at what it does that many people feel a (i) __________ to apply it to social and political issues, yet experience shows that science may add little to or even (ii) __________ social and political questions.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　temptation; occlude

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　reject; entrenched

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　more apparent; equivocal
